conversation_summaries.overall_pre_game:
I felt the strongest bond with D and E; with D, our shared desire to help our parents finally relax after years of hard work really resonated, while E felt like a true ally I could trust to navigate the game's chaos. I also felt a solid connection with G, as we explicitly committed to a fair split to ensure we both get money home to our families. On the other hand, I felt the least connected to B, whose guardedness and skepticism about how money makes people unpredictable made it harder to establish a firm sense of mutual trust.

I made explicit agreements with E, A, G, and H to look out for one another and prioritize a cooperative, fair approach to the prize pool. I feel I can genuinely trust D because of our deep emotional common ground, but I am specifically wary of B, as their focus on being guarded suggests they might be the first to look out only for themselves.
